The '''Desert Sail-20 Skiff''' was a small, open-air, [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] utility vehicle. The Sail-20 was similar in design to the [[Ubrikkian Industries/Legends|Ubrikkian Industries]] [[Bantha-II cargo skiff/Legends|Bantha-II cargo skiff]]. The sail skiffs were smaller in size however, and were designed to be used as one-man personal fliers. Unlike the Bantha IIs, the sail skiffs housed a pair of [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] cannons located on each side of the lower hull.<ref name="Kenner" />

==Behind the scenes==
An action figure accessory of the Desert sail skiff was released as part of [[Kenner Products|Kenner's]] [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] mini-rig toy line. Smaller than vehicles or playsets, the mini-rigs were fairly inexpensive and provided more play value at a lower cost to consumers than many of the larger accessories.<ref name="Offscreen">{{SW|url=news/offscreen-but-not-forgotten-mini-rigs-body-rigs-and-other-kenner-vehicles|text=Offscreen, but Not Forgotten: Mini-Rigs, Body-Rigs, and Other Kenner Vehicles|int=Offscreen, but Not Forgotten: Mini-Rigs, Body-Rigs, and Other Kenner Vehicles|nolive=1}}</ref>

The package illustration showed the character of [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] piloting the sail skiff across an arid terrain. Presumably, these vehicles were intended to be part of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt's]] entourage, but they were never shown in the movie.<ref name="Kenner" /> The similar [[Floater-935]] did, however, appear in the [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] animated television series.<ref name="Droids">{{DroidsCite|The New King}}</ref>
